data.title: Eden Innovations Halts Trading Ahead of Defence-Focused Expansion and Capital Raising
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Eden Innovations ( (AU:EDE) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Eden Innovations has requested that trading in its securities on the ASX be halted pending an announcement on the establishment of a new division focused on military, defence and infrastructure, alongside a capital raising. The halt will remain in place until the earlier of the company’s forthcoming announcement or the resumption of normal trading on 17 April 2026, signalling a potentially significant strategic expansion and financing move that could reshape its market positioning and funding base.

The company expects the trading halt to end once it releases full details of the capital raising and the new business division and has stated it is unaware of any reason the halt should not be granted. This step indicates Eden is preparing for a notable corporate development that may alter its operational focus and investor outlook, particularly as it targets defence-related markets and associated infrastructure opportunities.

More about Eden Innovations

Eden Innovations Ltd is an Australian-listed company based in Perth, Western Australia, whose securities trade on the ASX under the ticker EDE. The company operates in the broader technology and infrastructure sector and is now positioning itself to expand into military, defence and infrastructure-related activities through a newly planned business division.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

data.title: Great Northern Minerals Secures Nevada Gold-Silver Project with Strong Resource Upside
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Great Northern Minerals Limited ( (AU:GNM) ) has provided an update.

Great Northern Minerals has signed a binding exploration lease and option agreement to acquire 100% of the Iron Butte gold-silver project in Nevada’s Battle Mountain-Cortez district, a premier U.S. mining belt. The epithermal deposit shows a 500m by 300m mineralised footprint from surface to about 200m depth, with wide oxide gold-silver intercepts from 148 historical drill holes and indications of Carlin-style high-grade potential at depth.

The company is compiling historical data to deliver a maiden Inferred JORC-compliant mineral resource in the near term and plans exploration and infill drilling in Q3 2026 to grow the resource. Backed by a $4.2 million cash position after a recent divestment, Great Northern Minerals aims to rapidly advance Iron Butte, highlighting the potential for low-cost heap leach development and additional upside from previously underappreciated silver mineralisation in a strong gold and silver price environment.

More about Great Northern Minerals Limited

Great Northern Minerals Limited is an Australia-listed exploration company focused on precious metals, with a growing portfolio of gold and silver assets. The company targets high-potential districts in tier-one mining jurisdictions, seeking near-surface oxide resources with scalability and low-cost development pathways to enhance its position in the gold-silver sector.

Average Trading Volume: 852,155

data.title: Rivco lifts NAV and lease commitments amid subdued water entitlement trading
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Duxton Water Ltd. ( (AU:RIV) ) is now available.

Rivco reported a modest rise in its unaudited pre-tax net asset value to $1.80 per share in March, driven mainly by higher valuations for high security water entitlements across key trading zones, even as the closing share price remained at $1.43. The company continued to build contracted income by executing a new 1,000 megalitre lease, lifting its committed lease and forward percentage to 68% from July 2026, while operating in a subdued entitlement transaction market influenced by government buyback tenders and facing slightly weaker Southern Basin dam storage levels and spot water prices around $390 to $400 per megalitre.

Lower Murray spot prices ended March near $390 per megalitre before edging to about $400 by mid-April, reflecting ongoing tightness despite softer demand, and Southern Basin dam storages slipped by a weighted average of 1% during the month. Allocation percentages for high security entitlements remained relatively strong across the Goulburn, Murray and Murrumbidgee systems at the start of April, which, combined with Rivco’s expanding lease book, underpins cash flow stability for investors despite subdued trading volumes in the broader water entitlement market.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:RIV) stock is a Buy with a A$1.65 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Duxton Water Ltd. stock, see the AU:RIV Stock Forecast page.

More about Duxton Water Ltd.

Rivco Australia Limited, listed on the ASX, operates in the water rights and irrigation asset sector, holding a $304 million portfolio of water assets and 60.5 gigalitres of water entitlements. The company generates income primarily through leasing these entitlements, with 52% of the portfolio currently leased and a weighted average lease expiry of 3.1 years, supporting a gross dividend yield of 7.4%.

Average Trading Volume: 124,463

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: A$231.9M

Shine Justice Ltd. ( (AU:SHJ) ) has issued an announcement.

Shine Justice has announced that Simon Morrison will transition from Managing Director to Executive Director to concentrate on driving the group’s international growth agenda, particularly its International Mass Torts program. Group CEO Carolyn Barker will continue to lead overall strategy and operations, as the company seeks to grow its domestic class actions segment while accelerating international mass torts through an expanding global network, which the board believes will strengthen its competitive positioning and maintain stable day-to-day management.

The company views the International Mass Torts market as a significant growth avenue, leveraging its specialist capability in prosecuting class action matters efficiently in Australia. By enhancing its international partnerships and referral networks, Shine aims to broaden its pipeline of cross-border cases and capitalise on rising global focus on product and consumer harm issues, reinforcing its strategic shift towards more international-led growth.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:SHJ) stock is a Buy with a A$0.95 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Shine Justice Ltd. stock, see the AU:SHJ Stock Forecast page.

More about Shine Justice Ltd.

Shine Justice Ltd is an Australian legal services group focused on class actions and related litigation. The company is expanding its International Mass Torts program through global partnerships and referral networks, targeting growth opportunities driven by increased global scrutiny on product and consumer harm.

data.title: Pony AI Launches 2026 Equity Grants to Strengthen Talent and Incentives
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Pony AI Inc. ( (HK:2026) ) has shared an update.

Pony AI Inc. has granted nearly 7 million restricted share units and 15,000 options under its 2026 Share Scheme to over 500 employees, three independent non-executive directors and a service provider, representing a small percentage of its issued share capital. The awards, granted at no purchase price and vesting over one to two years subject to time-based and performance conditions, are designed to retain talent, align management and staff incentives with shareholders, and reinforce the company’s long-term growth strategy in the competitive autonomous driving industry.

The bulk of the RSUs will vest gradually for employees starting from anniversaries in late 2025 and March 2026, with senior management receiving a structured four-year-style vesting schedule tied to a March 2026 start date. Grants to directors fully vest after one year and the service provider’s allocation is contingent on performance targets over a two-year window, signaling Pony AI’s emphasis on performance-linked compensation and deeper engagement with key external partners.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2026) stock is a Buy with a HK$148.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Pony AI Inc. stock, see the HK:2026 Stock Forecast page.

More about Pony AI Inc.

Pony AI Inc. is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong and the U.S., operating in the autonomous driving and smart mobility technology sector. The company focuses on developing and commercializing self-driving solutions and related services, targeting global markets through dual listings and strategic technology partnerships.

data.title: Largo Boosts Vanadium Output and Seeks Multi-Metal Expansion at Maracás Mine
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Largo Resources ( (TSE:LGO) ) has issued an update.

Largo Inc. reported a sharp operational rebound in the first quarter of 2026, with vanadium pentoxide production more than doubling to 2,616 tonnes and ore mined rising nearly 91%, while sales volumes of vanadium and ilmenite both increased. Management highlighted operational improvements at the Maracás Menchen Mine and a stronger base to capitalize on higher U.S. vanadium prices and reduced U.S. tariff barriers, supporting plans to grow high-purity and ferrovanadium sales.

The company also moved to broaden its revenue mix by filing with Brazil’s mining regulator to authorize production and sale of copper, platinum group metals, nickel and cobalt as by-products using existing infrastructure, following encouraging flotation test results from its ilmenite plant. In parallel, Largo streamlined its senior leadership, naming Luis Rendón as sole chief operating officer and promoting Luânder Peixoto to group general counsel, underscoring a continued focus on operational turnaround and governance.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:LGO) stock is a Buy with a C$3.10 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Largo Resources stock, see the TSE:LGO Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on LGO Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, LGO is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (multi-year losses and poor free-cash-flow generation, including cash burn in 2025), with only partial support from an improved balance sheet (no debt). Technicals add mild headwinds (below key moving averages and negative MACD), while valuation is difficult to assess given a negative P/E and no dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on LGO stock, click here.

More about Largo Resources

Largo Inc., listed in Toronto and on Nasdaq, is the world’s largest primary vanadium producer, operating the Maracás Menchen Mine in Brazil. The company focuses on high-purity vanadium products for aerospace and other specialized markets, and also supplies the U.S. ferrovanadium market, where qualified sources remain limited.

data.title: Fin Resources Confirms Extensive Sulphide System at Cabin Lake’s Arrow Prospect
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Fin Resources Limited ( (AU:FIN) ) is now available.

Fin Resources has reported strong progress at its Cabin Lake Gold Project, confirming substantial sulphide-bearing banded iron formation at the Arrow Prospect through three diamond drillholes totaling about 114 metres of cumulative sulphide mineralisation. The company has also drilled its first hole at the nearby Beaver Prospect and is integrating ground magnetics, downhole IP and surface IP data to refine its model and prioritise future drilling.

The early results support continuity and scale of the mineralised system, with visual sulphide characteristics consistent with previously reported high-grade gold zones at Cabin Lake. Management says the drilling and geophysics program is rapidly improving understanding of what may be a continuous and extensive mineralised corridor, guiding a more targeted campaign ahead of a broader, data-driven summer exploration phase that could materially influence the project’s development trajectory.

More about Fin Resources Limited

Fin Resources Limited is an Australian exploration company focused on gold projects, with a key asset at the Cabin Lake Gold Project in Canada’s Northwest Territories. The company targets high-grade gold mineralisation hosted in banded iron formations and is advancing the project through drilling and integrated geophysical surveys to define a potentially extensive mineralised corridor.

Average Trading Volume: 4,446,707

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$15.38M

Wisetech Global ( (AU:WTC) ) has issued an announcement.

WiseTech Global has reported a minor change in the shareholdings of director Christopher Charlton and his related parties, covering both direct and indirect interests held personally, jointly with his spouse, and via nominee structures. The transactions, conducted on 10 April 2026 at $42.10 per share, involve small incremental purchases of ordinary shares and signal only a modest adjustment to the director’s overall equity position rather than a material shift in ownership or governance dynamics.

The update details increases across several holding lines, including shares held directly, jointly with Rachel Charlton, and through J P Morgan Nominees Australia on behalf of both individuals, as well as a new small holding in Rachel Charlton’s own name. While limited in scale, such disclosures maintain transparency around insider holdings for investors and regulators, reinforcing ongoing compliance with ASX director interest reporting requirements and offering stakeholders incremental insight into board members’ financial alignment with the company.

data.title: Quartzsea Acquisition Delays Latest Quarterly Filing
data.description:
Quartzsea Acquisition Corporation ( (QSEA) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Quartzsea Acquisition Corporation has notified regulators that it will be late filing its Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) for the quarter ended February 28, 2026. The company submitted a Form 12b-25 stating that the quarterly filing could not be completed by the April 14, 2026 deadline.

Management attributes the delay to extra time needed to complete the financial statements included in the Form 10-Q (Quarter Report). The company did not cite auditor changes, control failures, or investigations, framing the issue as a timing and workload problem rather than a structural or compliance concern.

Quartzsea expects to file the delayed Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) within the five-calendar-day extension window allowed under SEC rules. For investors, this points to a relatively short postponement rather than an open-ended delay, which generally reduces uncertainty compared with more serious filing disruptions.

The company indicated it does not anticipate any significant change in its results of operations compared with the same quarter a year earlier. No preliminary financial figures were provided, and all expectations remain forward-looking and subject to change once the full Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) is filed.

Quartzsea confirmed that all its other required periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed on time, signaling an otherwise clean compliance record. The late-filing notification was signed on April 14, 2026 by Chief Executive Officer Qi Gong, underscoring management’s responsibility for bringing the company back into full reporting compliance quickly.

More about Quartzsea Acquisition Corporation

Average Trading Volume: 2,748

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy
